title: Fix Beads closeout workflow contradictions and clarify tracked metadata paths
description:
During beans-zpt.1 closeout, the repo's Beads workflow contradicted itself in several places: the task worktree carried an invalid .beads redirect warning, dev/land-the-plane failed on untracked files that were legitimate task output, Beads metadata updates dirtied the root main checkout after dev/beads-finish, and the prune helper did not remove a closed task worktree. The repo guidance also uses imprecise terms like durable/non-durable when what matters operationally is which metadata file is the authoritative tracked export and which files are only local working state.

Fix the helpers, checks, and docs so a normal Beads task can be started, closed, landed, synced, and pruned without manual repair steps. Make the language explicit about which metadata file is authoritative, which files are just local checkout state, and which branch/worktree must contain the committed export.
acceptance_criteria:
- dev/beads-start, dev/beads-finish, dev/land-the-plane, and dev/beads-prune-closed-worktrees are audited against the actual repo worktree layout and updated where needed
- The closeout path no longer dirties root main in a way that blocks landing, or land-the-plane intentionally tolerates/cleans that state
- Worktree redirect wiring is verified so task worktrees do not emit misleading missing-.beads warnings
- The prune helper reliably removes closed worktrees like worktrees/beans-<issue-id>
- Docs replace vague durable/non-durable wording with explicit statements about authoritative tracked export files, local working files, and required commits/pushes
- Smoke checks cover the failure modes seen during beans-zpt.1 closeout

title: Migrate Codex memories into Rulesync and set Rulesync as AI config system of record
description: Import all existing guidance from .codex/memories into .rulesync/rules using rulesync import workflows, reconcile overlaps, and ensure generated downstream AI config files preserve current behavior. Add explicit project guidance that Rulesync is the system of record for AI coding configuration, analogous to Beads for task tracking.
acceptance_criteria: 1) All active .codex/memories guidance is represented in .rulesync/rules with no loss of critical instructions. 2) AGENTS and generated tool-specific outputs clearly state Rulesync as the AI config system of record. 3) Document the migration/update workflow for future rule changes (edit rulesync, then generate). 4) Validate generation output and confirm no accidental rule regressions.
close_reason: Superseded: migration already landed on origin/main; removing stale local worktree.

title: Create bean log entry for 2026-03-11 Guatemala brew
description:
Create a bean log entry for Wednesday, March 11, 2026 at 5:45 AM for the Guatemala coffee session.

Capture these brew details in the entry:
- pre-heated 311.7 g purified water
- 16.0 g beans measured
- 1.7 g grounds left over in the catchment and moved to a cup as a new technique so freshly ground coffee can be prioritized; if the target dose comes up short, pull some of the surplus back in with a spoon
- include or resolve a name for that technique, with "spare grounds cupping" as a candidate
- Timemore grinder setting 1.8.1
- 16.0 g grounds used for the brew
- 256.5 g brew water, steaming and not sizzling
- Easy Immersion recipe with 2'45" immersion and 3'45" total brew time

Follow the repo's bean-entry template and content conventions when turning these notes into the final markdown entry.
acceptance_criteria:
- A new bean entry exists for 2026-03-11 at 05:45 with the Guatemala coffee session details captured accurately
- The entry records the preheat water, dose, grinder setting, brew water, and Easy Immersion timing details from the notes
- The new catchment-surplus technique is described clearly, including either a chosen name or explicit mention of the naming question
- The content follows the bean entry schema and template conventions under src/content/beans/
- The resulting entry renders on the log route using the site's /beans/ base prefix, with the current expected preview path /beans/log/2026-03-11-bean1/

title: Add /scan-for-questions Rulesync command for bean entries
description:
Add a Rulesync command `/scan-for-questions <bean-entry-post-reference-or-slug>` that scans a bean entry for coffee-related questions, files each detected question into its own question page, and replaces the original in-post question with a filed-question link.

The command should accept either a bean entry slug or a full post reference such as `/beans/log/2026-03-10-bean1/`.

For each detected coffee-related question:
- create a dedicated question page under the Beans questions area
- include the question text on that page
- include a backreference to the originating bean post
- replace the original question in the source post with `Filed [<question text>](<question page link>).`

Example target behavior:
- input: `/scan-for-questions /beans/log/2026-03-10-bean1/`
- detect the question about when to clean the Timemore grinder
- create a question page like `beans/questions/when-to-clean-grinder/`
- link the source post to that dedicated question page
acceptance_criteria:
- The command accepts either a bean post reference or bean slug.
- Coffee-related questions in the target post are identified and extracted.
- Each extracted question gets a dedicated question page with the question text and a backreference to the originating bean post.
- The original question in the bean post is replaced with `Filed [<question text>](<question page link>).`
- Running the command on `/beans/log/2026-03-10-bean1/` files the Timemore grinder cleaning question into a dedicated question page and updates the source post accordingly.

title: Simplify bean log identifiers for single-entry days
description:
Research summary:
- Current bean log display text and navigation hard-code `beanKey` across the homepage stream, detail page prompt/title/nav, companion logbook, coffee detail references, and the bean-rendering check.
- Bean content filenames and frontmatter IDs currently use `<date>-<beanKey>`; Astro slugs therefore expose `-bean1` even when the day has only one entry.
- Existing multi-cup days all have a top-level `time` value, so disambiguation can prefer 24-hour time when there is more than one entry on the same date, with numeric fallback reserved for missing-time cases.
- The current command template in the bean-entry guidance still requires `$ bean log <YYYY-MM-DD> <beanKey>`, so docs/content conventions will need a follow-up update when implementation begins.

Implementation intent:
- Omit the trailing identifier from default bean log commands and labels when a date has exactly one bean entry.
- When a date has multiple bean entries, prefer a military-time disambiguator derived from the entry time; if time is unavailable, fall back to a simple ordinal such as `1` or `2`.
- Preserve stable routing for existing entries or define a redirect/back-compat plan before changing public slugs.
design: Likely implementation starts in shared bean data helpers by grouping beans per date and deriving a display/route identifier from collision count plus `time`. The issue should avoid leading with a content migration until a route-compat strategy is chosen.
acceptance_criteria:
- Document the chosen identifier rules for command text, labels, and URLs before coding.
- Identify and update all affected rendering surfaces: homepage stream, bean detail page prompt/title/nav, companion references, coffee detail references, and relevant checks/tests.
- Decide whether existing content files/frontmatter keep `beanKey` internally, migrate to a new field, or derive identifiers per date at build time.
- If public slugs change, include backward-compatibility handling for existing `/beans/log/<date>-beanN/` links.

title: Make h3 visually distinct from h4 with clear hierarchy
description:
Adjust markdown content heading styles so h3 and h4 are clearly distinguishable and convey hierarchy.

Context: Prior visual-distinction tasks include beans-2xn (inline code/backticks) and beans-2xl (blockquote styling). Reuse the same approach: improve distinction while preserving readability and contrast.

Idea to evaluate: render heading markers as a presentational cue, e.g. h3 appears with a prefixed '### ' token and h4 with '#### ' token (visual-only; semantic heading levels must remain h3/h4 in the HTML).

Acceptance criteria:
- h3 and h4 are visually distinct in bean markdown content.
- There is a clear visual hierarchy between h3 and h4 (size, weight, spacing, or marker treatment).
- Contrast/readability remain acceptable.
- Existing CLI-inspired tone is preserved.
- Add/adjust preview evidence for a /beans/ route showing both heading levels.
close_reason: Implemented h3/h4 hierarchy styling, added CLI marker treatment, and captured /beans/ preview evidence

title: Add list subcommand routes and redirect collection roots
description: Add explicit list routes for collection-style entities so the simulated CLI commands and the URL structure have parity. For example, if the page chrome says 'bean companion list', the collection entry route should live at /beans/companion/list/ and /beans/companion/ should redirect there.
acceptance_criteria:
- Add list routes for collection index pages where the UI presents a CLI-style list command
- Redirect collection roots such as /beans/companion/ to their /beans/<entity>/list/ counterpart when appropriate
- Keep detail routes and internal navigation working after the redirect/list split
- Verify the simulated CLI command text matches the routed URL structure

acceptance_criteria:

Set up Playwright MCP server, ensure Chromium install, and document/automate devcontainer startup. Include guidance for using container-local http://localhost and screenshots/fullPage.

title: Add markdown accessibility linting for content entries
description: Introduce a content lint step that checks markdown accessibility requirements across all entries (beans, equipment, roasters, recipes). Validate that every markdown image has non-empty alt text and that heading levels follow the expected hierarchy (H2 section headers, H3 subsection headers, no skipped levels).
acceptance_criteria: - CI runs the a11y lint on PRs and fails on violations\n- Image syntax requires non-empty alt text (e.g., ![Alt](/beans/images/...) )\n- Heading structure enforces H2 then H3 (no H1/H4 skips) and flags out-of-order headings

title: Add editorial title triage skill for weak bean entry titles
description: Create a Rulesync-backed AI skill that reviews bean entry titles, identifies the 10 least interesting titles in the archive, and then updates those posts by selecting a more compelling quoted phrase from each post body. The target sensibility is the kind of juicy, high-signal pull-quote an editor at The Economist, Harper's Bazaar, The New Yorker, or The New York Times might appreciate: specific, vivid, and earned by the text rather than clickbait. Replacement titles must be all lowercase and no more than five words. Use the March 2, 2026 bean post (`2026-03-02-bean1`) as the style reference for title form. The workflow should make the selection process reproducible enough that future agents can rerun it on demand and then apply the resulting title edits to the chosen entries.
acceptance_criteria:
- A local Rulesync-managed skill exists that tells Codex when and how to identify the 10 weakest or least interesting bean entry titles
- The skill defines a practical rubric for weak-title detection and for choosing stronger replacement quotes from the post body
- Replacement titles are derived from the post text, are all lowercase, and are no more than five words
- The workflow uses the March 2, 2026 bean post (`2026-03-02-bean1`) as the reference for title form
- The workflow includes updating the selected bean entries with improved titles derived from their own text
- The quoted replacements preserve the voice of the posts and avoid fabricated wording
- Any generated agent outputs are refreshed with `npx rulesync generate` if the source of truth lives under `.rulesync/`
- Content validation/build passes after the title updates

title: Codify enqueue intent: create Beads task and commit issues.jsonl on main
description: Define and document a strict workflow rule: adding or updating an issue in Beads is not complete until that metadata is committed in git. No commit means the enqueue action is incomplete and operationally useless. Default path is to sync/export tracked issues.jsonl and commit on main. Exception: when operating inside an active task worktree branch, committing the metadata update on that worktree branch is acceptable, with expected merge/landing to main later.
acceptance_criteria:
- Written guidance in AGENTS or docs states this hard rule explicitly: no commit means not done
- Workflow includes creating/updating the issue with bd in direct mode
- Workflow includes syncing/exporting Beads metadata to tracked issues.jsonl
- Default behavior commits metadata changes to main with a clear commit message
- Exception behavior allows committing metadata on an active task worktree branch, with explicit expectation that it lands in main via normal merge flow
- Completion criteria requires evidence of a git commit containing the issue metadata update
- Verification step confirms the issue ID is present in the committed issues.jsonl on the branch where enqueue is completed
- Add a short operator checklist or helper command reference to prevent future ambiguity
notes:
Requested by user on 2026-03-06 after observing task creation without an explicit main-branch metadata commit expectation.
Exception added per user guidance on 2026-03-06: in a worktree context, metadata commit may be made on the worktree branch and land in main later via normal merge.
User clarification on 2026-03-06: adding something to Beads without a git commit is completely useless.

title: Add verification for list-only homepage and detail navigation
description: Verify /beans/ shows list-only entries and no embedded post body content, with working links to /beans/log/<slug> detail pages. Include preview screenshot for Codex and note expected 429 risk reduction from fewer embeds on index.

title: Fix recent brew log backlinks rendering empty titles on coffee pages
close_reason: Fixed coffee recent brew log backlinks and labels

title: Fix check:bean-rendering date-header assertion
description: The bean rendering check fails after adding 2026-02-22-bean2 with 'Bean detail page is missing the expected date-only header'. Update the check so it validates stable structure without brittle assumptions.

title: Fix reversed prev/next bean log navigation
description: Bean detail pages currently wire the adjacent navigation backwards: Prev points newer and Next points older. Update /beans/log/<slug>/ so Prev goes to the older entry and Next goes to the newer entry, then verify with a detail-page preview.
notes: Fixed reversed bean log prev/next chronology and verified detail navigation preview.

title: Harden Beads daemon and startup mode for local/worktree sessions
description: Investigate and fix unreliable Beads daemon behavior in this repo so common startup commands do not hang in local or worktree sessions. Observations from 2026-03-01: bd show beans-1rz hung until rerun with --no-daemon; dev/beads-start also stalled because it shells through bd commands without forcing direct mode; .git write failures were separate sandbox issues, but the daemon/direct-storage behavior itself appeared flaky independent of that. Scope: inspect .beads daemon setup, hooks, socket/lock behavior, and repo docs; decide whether repo helpers should always force --no-daemon or whether daemon configuration can be made reliable; document the chosen policy and update startup helpers accordingly.
acceptance_criteria:
- Root cause of the hanging daemon-path behavior is documented
- Repo startup helpers use the reliable mode by default
- Docs explain when to use daemon vs --no-daemon in this repo
- Starting work on a bead does not hang in typical local/worktree sessions
notes: Reproduced on 2026-03-01: beans-e2z existed in .beads/beads.db and via bd show, but was absent from both the main checkout .beads/issues.jsonl and the beads-sync worktree JSONL. Plain bd commands hung intermittently until rerun with --no-daemon. A stale daemon then re-imported from beads-sync in a loop and reverted exports. Repo helpers/docs now force direct mode; durable export should be verified against .git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync/.beads/issues.jsonl.

title: Add person tags to bean posts and backfill the archive
description: Start tagging people mentioned in bean posts with a canonical tag format like `Person/<FirstName> <LastName>`. Apply this both prospectively and retroactively across the existing archive. Include musicians being listened to, composers/performers referenced in embeds or prose, and other explicitly referenced people mentioned in the posts. As part of the work, define how person tags should coexist with the existing listening/topic tag patterns and normalize casing/spacing expectations for the new tag family.
acceptance_criteria:
- A canonical person-tag format is defined and documented through implementation, using `Person/<FirstName> <LastName>` or an explicitly approved variant if constraints require adjustment
- Existing bean posts are reviewed and backfilled with person tags where named people are referenced
- Listening-related people such as musicians, composers, and performers are included when they are named in the post
- Non-music referenced people named in prose are also tagged when they are clearly identifiable
- The approach avoids ambiguous tags for partially identified people or explains the fallback rule for those cases

title: Update Beads helper scripts for current bd CLI
description: The repo's Beads helper scripts have drifted from the installed bd CLI. Current breakages observed during beans-fji closeout: git commit and dev/beads-start hit an unsupported 'bd hook' subcommand via the repo's hook path, and dev/beads-finish/dev/land-the-plane still call 'bd sync --force', which is no longer supported by the installed bd version. Update the helper scripts and any related checks/docs so the repo's standard Beads startup and landing flows work end-to-end again without manual workarounds like --no-verify, direct bd sync commands, or helper bypasses.
acceptance_criteria:
- Starting a task with dev/beads-start no longer fails because of an unsupported bd hook path
- Committing in the repo no longer requires --no-verify to bypass broken Beads hook integration
- dev/beads-finish and dev/land-the-plane work with the installed bd CLI and no longer call unsupported flags
- Related smoke checks/docs/rules are updated to match the supported bd workflow
- A Beads-managed task can be started, committed, finished, and landed without the manual compatibility workarounds used during beans-fji closeout
notes: Resolved in codex/beans-jqm while landing beans-jqm: dev/beads-start now refreshes shared git hooks with 'bd hooks install --force'; dev/beads-finish now uses supported bd 0.42.0 flags ('--notes' plus explicit export to .git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync/.beads/issues.jsonl); dev/land-the-plane no longer calls unsupported 'bd sync --force'; smoke checks/docs/rules were updated and npm run check:beads-start, check:beads-finish, check:land-the-plane, and build all passed.

Implement an RSS-safe rich formatting simulation (for example: aside/figure/blockquote/cite/heading structure plus optional conservative inline style) so comments feel visually distinct without relying on site CSS or scripts. Semantics must make the content origin obvious to humans and assistive tech: this is a virtual companion comment, not the author main body.
acceptance_criteria:

notes: Scope update on 2026-03-06: keep companion comments in RSS, but improve semantic clarity and visual distinction with feed-reader-safe markup. Prior concern was confusion in Readwise when comment text looked like regular post body.

title: Requirements intake for Questions content space
description:
Run a focused requirements interview to define the top-level Questions content space in Beans.

Scope:
- Confirm information architecture for Questions content and how it differs from existing Beans content types.
- Define minimum metadata, including required date-created semantics for questions.
- Define CLI-style command patterns that surface an individual question.
- Confirm how Questions should appear on the front page reverse-chron timeline and tie-breaking rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- Interview transcript captured in issue notes.
- Decision summary includes required fields for Questions.
- Decision summary includes slug strategy and canonical URL patterns under /beans/.
- Decision summary includes CLI command format examples for Questions.
- Implementation-ready checklist produced for follow-on tasks.
notes:
Refactored scope: Terms is complete and tracked outside this issue; this issue now covers Questions-only requirements.

Requirements intake transcript (repo review)
- Reviewed current Astro content model in `src/content/config.ts` and the existing content spaces under `src/content/`.
- Reviewed homepage structure in `src/pages/index.astro` and reverse-chron bean sorting in `src/data/beans.ts`.
- Reviewed current CLI detail/index command patterns in `src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro`, `src/components/BeanEntry.astro`, `src/pages/log/[slug].astro`, and `src/pages/terms/[slug].astro`.
- Reviewed Terms date handling in `src/data/terms.ts` and `src/pages/terms/[slug].astro` to distinguish filesystem-derived reference dates from authored content dates.
- Reviewed dependent implementation issue `beans-4zb` to keep the requirements scoped to the next implementation slice.

Decision summary for Questions

1. Information architecture
- Add Questions as a new top-level content collection named `questions`, parallel to `beans`, `terms`, `recipes`, `equipment`, and `roasters`.
- Questions are not bean-log entries and should not live under `/log/`; each Question is its own standalone content page with markdown body content.
- Questions differ from Terms in two important ways:
  - Questions are authored timeline content and should participate in the homepage reverse-chron stream.
  - Terms are evergreen reference entries sorted alphabetically and currently derive dates from filesystem metadata.
- Questions differ from Beans in that they are lighter-weight, do not require brew/coffee metadata, and should be keyed by semantic slug rather than date + bean key.
- Recommended routes:
  - index: `/beans/questions/`
  - detail: `/beans/questions/<slug>/`
- Recommended index command: `bean questions`
- Recommended detail command namespace: `bean question <slug>`

2. Required metadata
- Minimum required frontmatter for a Question entry:
  - `title: string`
  - `summary: string`
  - `dateCreated: string`
- Optional metadata can be added later only if implementation proves it necessary, but the first pass should avoid inventing bean-like fields.
- The markdown body remains the primary content payload and should hold the actual question framing, context, and any evolving answer/discussion.

3. Date-created semantics
- `dateCreated` should be authored frontmatter, not inferred from filesystem timestamps.
- Format should be ISO calendar date `YYYY-MM-DD`, matching existing Beans date conventions.
- The detail page may render the label as `date-created` to match the CLI/reference presentation style, but the content schema/frontmatter key should stay camelCase (`dateCreated`) to match repo conventions such as `beanKey` and `purchaseDate`.
- Do not reuse the Terms filesystem-date helper for Questions; Questions need stable authored chronology that survives file moves, rebases, and backfills.

4. Slug strategy and canonical URLs
- Use semantic kebab-case filenames/slugs with no leading date prefix, for example `why-use-rdt.md` or `when-to-rest-light-roasts.md`.
- Canonical detail URLs should be `/beans/questions/<slug>/`.
- Canonical index URL should be `/beans/questions/`.
- Keep the slug stable even if `dateCreated` is corrected later; chronology belongs to metadata, not to the filename.
- Use the repo's existing `withBase` helper so local previews and links resolve under the `/beans/` base prefix.

5. CLI-style command examples for individual questions
- `bean questions`
- `bean question why-use-rdt`
- `bean question when-to-rest-light-roasts`
- For page chrome and homepage item rendering, display the simulated command using the singular detail form (`bean question <slug>`), because it is short, deterministic, and does not require quoting long natural-language titles.

6. Homepage reverse-chron ordering and tie-breaking
- Questions should join the existing homepage stream as timeline items, not a separate homepage section.
- Primary sort key: descending authored date (`dateCreated` for questions, `date` for beans).
- Secondary sort key when same-day comparison is needed:
  - timed bean entries sort ahead of untimed Question entries on the same date, because Beans already support intra-day ordering and Questions intentionally do not require a time field in v1.
- Final deterministic tie-break when same content type still collides:
  - beans: existing `time` descending behavior remains; if two bean entries still tie, fall back to slug ascending for determinism.
  - questions: slug ascending.
- This preserves a deterministic merged homepage order without expanding the initial Questions schema beyond the required `dateCreated` field.

7. Downstream implementation checklist unlocked by this intake
- Add `questions` collection schema in `src/content/config.ts` with required `title`, `summary`, and `dateCreated`.
- Add `src/content/questions/` with at least one sample entry using semantic filename slug.
- Add data helper(s) to read/sort questions by `dateCreated` descending.
- Add `/questions/` index page and `/questions/[slug].astro` detail page using existing BaseLayout conventions and `withBase`.
- Render `date-created` on Question detail pages from frontmatter.
- Add homepage integration that merges beans and questions with the ordering rules above.
- Add the Questions link to site navigation when the pages exist.

Blocking decisions needed from the user
- None discovered from local repo context. The current repo patterns are sufficient to implement the first Questions slice without further product clarification.

Concise closeout
- Proposed Questions as a standalone top-level timeline-aware content type.
- Chose explicit authored `dateCreated` frontmatter, semantic slug filenames, and canonical `/beans/questions/<slug>/` routes.
- Unlocked next tasks: collection/schema work in `beans-4zb`, then homepage stream integration in `beans-g7e`.

title: 26t1w09 worktree cleanup
description:
Cleanup and normalize the remaining local Beads/task git state in this repo after partial landing work completed on March 4, 2026.

Current known state as of 2026-03-04:
- Main checkout path: /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans
- Main branch: main
- Main commit after cleanup work: 6eff8de
- origin/main already includes commit 6eff8de
- The only attached non-main worktree is the persistent metadata worktree at /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans/.git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync on branch beads-sync
- beads-sync is the repo's Beads metadata branch and is not a normal feature/task branch to merge into main

What was already done on 2026-03-04:
- Loaded repo instructions from AGENTS.md
- Confirmed main was clean
- Confirmed beads-sync was the only attached task-related worktree
- Merged local branch codex/beans-1rz into main
- Pushed main to origin, resulting in main commit 6eff8de
- Deleted these safely ancestry-merged local branches with git branch -d:
  - codex/beans-1g2
  - codex/beans-1rz
  - codex/beans-7k3
  - codex/beans-swm

Branches/worktrees that still require follow-up:
- beads-sync (attached worktree)
- codex/beans-2wm (local branch, upstream exists at origin/codex/beans-2wm)
- codex/beans-7kp (local branch, no upstream shown)
- codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim (local branch, no upstream shown)

Important Beads issue context already checked:
- beans-e2z is CLOSED and corresponds to Beads daemon/direct-mode hardening work; beads-sync currently points at commit message "Sync Beads for beans-e2z"
- beans-1rz is CLOSED and was merged into main on 2026-03-04
- beans-2wm is CLOSED
- beans-7kp is CLOSED
- The remaining git branches are not necessarily safe to delete with plain git branch -d just because their Beads issues are CLOSED

Known analysis from 2026-03-04:
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-1rz showed one unapplied patch, which is why it was merged
- git merge-base --is-ancestor confirmed codex/beans-1g2, codex/beans-7k3, and codex/beans-swm were already merged into main before deletion
- codex/beans-2wm, codex/beans-7kp, and codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim are NOT ancestry-merged into main, so git branch -d would not be the right cleanup command unless their tips are first landed or you intentionally force-delete them
- Earlier symmetric-diff checks were misleading because main...branch reports branch-point-relative changes, not only tip-to-tip tree equality; re-check the actual tip trees before deciding

What the next agent should do:
1. Reconfirm the latest state with exact commands before making changes:
   - git status --short --branch
   - git worktree list
   - git branch -vv
   - bd --no-daemon list
   - bd --no-daemon show beans-2wm
   - bd --no-daemon show beans-7kp
2. Inspect each remaining branch relative to main using both ancestry and actual tree diff:
   - git merge-base --is-ancestor <branch> main
   - git log --oneline main..<branch>
   - git diff --stat main <branch>
   - git cherry -v main <branch>
3. Decide branch-by-branch whether the correct action is:
   - land it into main because it still contains desired work not in main
   - close/sync the related Beads metadata if the issue was not fully finished
   - remove the worktree if it is only leftover metadata/worktree state
   - delete the local branch safely with git branch -d if it becomes ancestry-merged
   - force-delete the local branch only if it is intentionally obsolete and the tip is confirmed not needed
4. For Beads-managed branches that still need a formal closeout, prefer the repo helpers described in AGENTS.md and docs/dev/beads.md:
   - use dev/beads-finish <issue-id> for close/sync when appropriate
   - use dev/land-the-plane <issue-id> from the task worktree when the task branch/worktree still exists in the expected form and should land into main
5. Be careful with beads-sync:
   - it is a persistent metadata branch/worktree, not a normal task branch
   - do not merge beads-sync into main
   - if cleanup includes deleting obsolete task worktrees, beads-sync should normally remain unless there is a very explicit repo-level reason to recreate it
6. If local/remote main has changed since 2026-03-04, use the repo's normal policy from AGENTS.md:
   - update local main from origin/main
   - merge the task branch into main locally
   - push main directly
7. After cleanup, re-run:
   - git worktree list
   - git branch -vv
   - bd --no-daemon ready
   - bd --no-daemon sync --check
   - bd --no-daemon sync --force (if metadata changes were made)

Open questions the next agent must resolve explicitly:
- Is codex/beans-2wm still needed as a distinct branch, or was its intent already superseded by later commits on main?
- Is codex/beans-7kp truly obsolete, or does its tip contain any content/config state that still needs to be preserved another way?
- Is codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim just a leftover local experiment/bean entry branch, or should that content be landed or archived elsewhere before deletion?
- Are there any stale remote branches that should also be cleaned up after local state is normalized?

Safety rules:
- Do not use destructive cleanup blindly
- Do not delete non-main branches solely because the associated Beads issue is CLOSED
- Verify branch tip content against current main before any forced delete
- Preserve the repo's main-first landing policy from AGENTS.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Remaining task/worktree branches are each either landed into main, intentionally preserved with rationale, or deleted with the correct git command
- No obsolete task worktrees remain attached after cleanup, except the persistent beads-sync metadata worktree if still required by repo policy
- main is synced with origin/main after any landing work
- Beads metadata is synced and verified when issue/workflow updates were made
- Final notes record what branches/worktrees were kept, landed, or deleted and why
notes:
Session evidence from 2026-03-04 cleanup pass:

Commands/results already observed:
- git worktree list showed:
  - /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans on main
  - /Users/pnore/Documents/GitHub/codekiln/beans/.git/beads-worktrees/beads-sync on beads-sync
- git branch -vv initially showed:
  - beads-sync at 0f3e919 with upstream origin/beads-sync
  - codex/beans-1g2 at abc1492
  - codex/beans-1rz at 5fa4894 with upstream origin/codex/beans-1rz
  - codex/beans-2wm at 2f09a17 with upstream origin/codex/beans-2wm
  - codex/beans-7k3 at e790ee2 with upstream origin/codex/beans-7k3: gone
  - codex/beans-7kp at b64abcf
  - codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim at f17854b
  - codex/beans-swm at 70b3c61 with upstream origin/codex/beans-swm
- git log --left-right main...beads-sync showed beads-sync differs from main only as Beads metadata/history, not as a task branch to merge into main
- git diff --name-status main...beads-sync showed only .beads/issues.jsonl
- bd --no-daemon list showed open/ready issues unrelated to this cleanup and confirmed Beads was functioning in direct mode
- bd --no-daemon show confirmed these issues were already CLOSED:
  - beans-e2z
  - beans-1rz
  - beans-2wm
  - beans-7kp

Branch-specific checks already run:

  + 5fa48945faed453ff143e32096123593437fef05 Add Dependabot and PR CI automation
  Conclusion: codex/beans-1rz still had one unapplied patch and should be landed.
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-2wm returned:

- git cherry -v main codex/beans-7kp returned:
  + cb6f703bd0c9df26c0811637a9184a4b587f9182 Fix optional bean attribute rendering and add regression check
  Conclusion at the time was ambiguous because other diff modes were misleading; re-check actual tip tree and commit containment before deleting.
- git cherry -v main codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim returned:
  - f17854b2d84de462d0ad3dbea2d9321953372caa bean entry 2026-02-16 mon cup1
  Conclusion: patch-equivalent content appears to exist on main, but branch tip is not ancestry-merged.
- git merge-base --is-ancestor confirmed these were already merged into main before deletion:
  - codex/beans-1g2
  - codex/beans-7k3
  - codex/beans-swm

Changes actually made on 2026-03-04:
- Ran:
  git fetch origin
  git pull --ff-only origin main
  git merge --no-edit codex/beans-1rz
  git push origin main
- Result: main advanced to commit 6eff8de

State after that landing pass:

Caution from the session:
- main...branch symmetric diffs caused confusion because they are relative to the merge base and can make already-superseded branches look active.
- Before deleting codex/beans-2wm, codex/beans-7kp, or codex/beans-qqd-install-neovim, prefer exact checks against current main:

title: Normalize entity collection routes to singular nouns
description: Audit Beans entity collection URLs and align collection/index routes to a singular naming scheme so the information architecture matches the CLI tone more consistently. The current site mixes plural collection roots like /questions/ and /roasters/ with singular roots like /companion/, which makes routing and command naming feel inconsistent.
acceptance_criteria:
- Decide and document the singular route convention for entity collection roots across Beans

title: Document source-of-truth workflow for Beads and code across environments
description:
Document a single, explicit workflow so AI agents and humans consistently handle task metadata vs code across all execution contexts (Codespaces, local devcontainer, Codex Desktop on work/home machines, and Codex Cloud).

Scope:
- Define source of truth for Beads metadata and product code.
- Explain expected branch/worktree behavior (including beads-sync usage).
- Add concise environment-agnostic startup/preflight instructions.
- Add clear do/don't examples for committing and syncing issue metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- AGENTS.md clearly states Beads metadata source of truth and code source of truth.
- README.md (or docs/dev/beads.md) includes a short operator-focused summary.
- Instructions include at least one concrete command sequence for starting work and syncing metadata.
- Guidance is explicit for all listed environments and does not conflict with existing Beads docs.
- A new contributor/agent can determine the correct commit target (working branch vs beads-sync) without ambiguity.
notes:
Implementation plan:
1. Audit existing guidance in AGENTS.md and docs/dev/beads.md for ambiguity.
2. Write a minimal canonical policy section (source-of-truth + commit targets).
3. Add a short preflight checklist and command examples.
4. Cross-check wording against current Beads behavior and update references.
document source-of-truth workflow

title: Add attribute history script and Rulesync skill for content forensics
description: Add a repo script that reports the history of a frontmatter attribute over time, similar to inspecting recent values of fields like `brew.method` across `src/content/beans/*.md`. The script should be usable from the terminal and a local Codex skill should guide agents to use it for questions about how an attribute has changed over time. Because this repo treats generated agent files as derived output, the durable source of truth should live under `.rulesync/` where appropriate, with any generated outputs refreshed via `npx rulesync generate`.
acceptance_criteria:
- A script exists in the repo that can print attribute values over time for content entries (for example, recent values for a frontmatter path)
- The script documentation or interface makes clear which content set/path formats are supported
- A local skill exists that tells Codex when and how to use the script for attribute-history questions
- If the skill or guidance is sourced from Rulesync-managed files, the generated agent outputs are refreshed with `npx rulesync generate`
- The workflow is documented enough that future agents do not need to rediscover it ad hoc

title: Teach dev/land-the-plane to prune closed task worktrees
description: Rulesync history says successful land-the-plane closeout should leave no closed-task Beads worktree under worktrees/, but the current dev/land-the-plane script never prunes the just-landed task worktree. Investigation: 4304b24 added the land-the-plane RuleSync command; 9dce078 added dev/beads-prune-closed-worktrees as a separate helper; 1ea9e85 updated the RuleSync command to require 'Prune closed-task worktrees with dev/beads-prune-closed-worktrees' and declared the final invariant that no closed-task Beads worktree should remain under worktrees/ after landing. Current script history through e82c3ae still merges/pushes and verifies final state without invoking the prune helper, which leaves repros like worktrees/beans-6od behind even though the Beads issue is closed and landed.
acceptance_criteria:
- dev/land-the-plane prunes the just-closed task worktree as part of successful closeout, or fails clearly if it cannot do so safely
- Final-state assertions include the no-closed-task-worktree invariant documented in Rulesync
- Smoke coverage proves closed task worktrees are removed during land-the-plane, not only by a separate manual helper
- Rulesync/docs/script behavior are aligned so operators are not told cleanup is complete while the closed task worktree still exists
notes:
Repro on 2026-03-08: after dev/land-the-plane beans-6od, the issue was closed and pushed, but worktrees/beans-6od still existed until manual pruning.
Auto-prune closed task worktrees during closeout

title: Overhaul header navigation into a mobile-friendly command menu
description:
The current header navigation wraps awkwardly as the command list grows. Redesign the site navigation so the header works cleanly across viewport sizes, with a mobile-friendly menu pattern that preserves the CLI-inspired Beans tone.

User intent for this epic:
- Replace the clunky wrapping header nav with a cleaner responsive navigation system
- Use the simulated prompt `🫘>` as the primary header/menu trigger instead of a generic hamburger icon
- Move the light/dark theme switch into the menu rather than leaving it exposed in the main header row
- Keep the command-oriented visual language and avoid losing discoverability on desktop
acceptance_criteria:
- Header navigation no longer wraps awkwardly at common viewport widths
- Mobile navigation uses the `🫘>` prompt motif as the primary menu trigger
- Theme switching is available inside the menu
- The final solution remains consistent with the site's CLI-inspired aesthetic and works across desktop and mobile
